---
title: "Mount Tlaloc: the Mexica Ritual Landscape"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false

library(leaflet)
library(sf)
library(gpx)

# 1. DATA PREP

hike_raw <- gpx::read_gpx("mt.tlaloc.gpx")
hike_df <- hike_raw$tracks[[1]]
hike_df <- hike_df[complete.cases(hike_df[, c("Longitude", "Latitude")]), ]
hike_sf <- st_as_sf(hike_df, coords = c("Longitude", "Latitude"), crs = 4326)
hike_coords <- as.data.frame(st_coordinates(hike_sf))

# 2. FOUR ARCHIVAL POINTS
poi_data <- data.frame(
  name = c("Mountain Base", 
           "Lower Zone (9,675 ft)", 
           "Upper Zone (11,983 ft)", 
           "Tetzacualo Summit"),
  elev = c("2,980m", "2,949m", "3,652m", "4,120m"),
  lat  = c(19.3533, 19.36291, 19.38932, 19.412),
  lng  = c(-98.6631, -98.67416, -98.70868, -98.712),
 
  file = c("index.html", "lower_ascent.html", "upper_ascent.html", "summit.html"),
  color = c("forestgreen", "cadetblue", "purple", "gold")
)

# 3.  INTERACTIVE MAP
m <- leaflet() %>%
  addProviderTiles(providers$Esri.WorldTopoMap) %>%
  
  # The Trail Path (Red line)
  addPolylines(
    lng = hike_coords$X, 
    lat = hike_coords$Y, 
    color = "#8B0000", 
    weight = 3, 
    opacity = 0.6
  ) %>%
  
  # The Archival Markers (Points)
  addCircleMarkers(
    data = poi_data,
    lng = ~lng, 
    lat = ~lat,
    color = "white",       # Border ring
    fillColor = ~color,    # Category fill color
    radius = 9,
    weight = 2,
    fillOpacity = 0.8,
    stroke = TRUE,
    popup = ~paste0(
      "<div style='font-family: serif;'>",
      "<b>", name, "</b><br>",
      "<i>Elevation: ", elev, "</i><br>",
      "<a href='", file, "'>Access Archival Documents</a>",
      "</div>"
    )
  )

# 4. DISPLAY MAP
m
```
